inputs.prompt: Newsletter: The Craft. Feedback (12 reader replies): 1) 'Love the contract red flags section. More of this please.' 2) 'Too US-focused. I'm in the UK and the rates don't apply to me.' 3) 'The freelance rates database is the only reason I subscribe.' 4) 'Can you cover ghostwriting? Nobody talks about it but half of us do it.' 5) 'Great newsletter but too long sometimes. I skip the links section.' 6) 'I shared the contract edition with every writer I know.' 7) 'More UK and European content please.' 8) 'Could you interview editors about what they actually want to see in pitches?' 9) 'The rates are helpful but I'd love more context on negotiation tactics.' 10) 'Please don't add a paid tier. I can barely afford my internet connection.' 11) 'Too much advice for magazine writers. What about content writers and copywriters?' 12) 'Your edition on kill fees changed my career. Not exaggerating.'

Feedback (8 survey responses for a podcast): 'Episodes too long.' 'Love the host's voice.' 'More interviews with scientists.' 'The ads are too frequent.' 'Great show.' 'Can you cover more biology topics?' 'Episodes too long — I skip the last 20 minutes.' 'The Spotify exclusive episodes are annoying.'

Newsletter: European Housing Watch. Feedback (10 reader messages): 1) 'Excellent coverage of the German Mietendeckel failure. More Germany please.' 2) 'Your Eastern European coverage is almost nonexistent. Poland's housing market is fascinating right now.' 3) 'Can you add a regular data visualization? The numbers are hard to parse in text form.' 4) 'The fortnightly schedule is perfect. Do not go weekly.' 5) 'Too academic sometimes. I'm a journalist, not a researcher.' 6) 'Could you cover housing co-operatives as a model? Vienna's system is underreported.' 7) 'Your piece on Barcelona's tourist flat ban was the best thing I read this month.' 8) 'I'd pay for a quarterly deep-dive report with original data.' 9) 'Please cover the UK post-Brexit housing divergence from EU standards.' 10) 'More interviews with policymakers, fewer literature reviews.'
